title: THE DEAD ENGINEER — DIRECTOR'S CUT V2

hale_death_correlation_03.title: DEATH RECORD CORRELATION
hale_death_correlation_03.description: Match the recovered records with the correct contradiction.

events.text: About channel 7.14...
Before we go anywhere near it, I need you to understand something.
Curiosity is how this station got most of its scars.
Scientists call it discovery. Engineers call it troubleshooting. Management calls it initiative.
The rest of us called it Tuesday.
Some signals should stay buried. Yes, I know how dramatic that sounds.
Unfortunately, space has made me very dramatic.
Also sleep deprivation. Never underestimate what three months of bad sleep can do to a man's sense of theatre.
You are going straight for the thing I asked you not to touch.
I respect the consistency. I hate the survival instinct.
7.14 was a backup emergency carrier. Quiet frequency, low traffic, ugly signal profile.
The sort of channel you build because regulations require redundancy, then hope you never need.
Fine. But if that channel answers us, remember this wasn't curiosity. It was a door handle.
Good. Records first.
I hate that the unknown voice gave useful advice. That feels unfair.
For the record, if a haunted signal starts giving better procedural guidance than I do, I am filing a formal complaint.
Assuming the complaint department survived.
[PERSONNEL CACHE RECOVERED]

events.title: PERSONNEL FILE: ETHAN HALE
events.content:
DR. ETHAN HALE
ROLE: CHIEF ENGINEER
ASSIGNMENT: ORBITAL RESEARCH STATION ECHO
STATUS: DECEASED
TIME OF DEATH: 21:20
LOCATION: DOOR 12 ACCESS CORRIDOR
events.caption: Recovered personnel record from corrupted station cache.

events.text: Operator?
events.speaker: HALE

events.text: Read that again.
events.speaker: HALE

events.text: No. Sorry. Don't read it again. I read it. I just needed the universe to make a different choice the second time.
events.speaker: HALE

events.text: Status: deceased.
events.speaker: HALE

events.text: I've been called a lot of things.
events.speaker: HALE

events.text: Chief Engineer. Workaholic. Condescending, once, by a man who used the word 'synergy' unironically.
events.speaker: HALE

events.text: Dead is new.
events.speaker: HALE

events.text: No. That file is wrong.
events.speaker: HALE

events.text: Or the system is wrong. Please let it be the system.
events.speaker: HALE

events.text: Do I sound dead to you?
events.speaker: HALE

events.text: That was not rhetorical. I am genuinely asking because my confidence in basic categories is collapsing.
events.speaker: HALE

events.text: Yes. I can read.
I am trying very hard not to think about what that means while still continuing to breathe.
Which, by the way, is an extremely inconvenient thing to do while looking at your own death certificate.
There should be a manual for this. 'So You've Been Officially Dead for Months.'
[HALE TRUST DEGRADED]
It could be.
I need it to be. That is not science. That is terror wearing a lab coat.
Records corrupt all the time. Timestamps drift. Personnel caches duplicate entries. Once the mess hall system listed me as a vegetarian for six weeks.
I survived that. Barely.
But this doesn't look like mess hall corruption.
I am here. I am speaking to you.
I can hear my own voice in the helmet return. I can feel my pulse in my teeth.
I am tired. I am scared. I am extremely irritated by the archive UI.
Dead people don't send messages.
Right?
That's how this works, yes?

events.speaker: UNKNOWN
events.text: HE NEVER ASKED
I really wish it had said something less horrible.
Also less accurate. It is developing a talent for being upsetting in very specific ways.

choices.text: WHAT DO YOU REMEMBER ABOUT DOOR 12?
TALK TO ME. WHAT DO YOU MISS?

events.text: I don't know.
No. That's not honest.
I know pieces. I just don't know if they're mine.
I remember the door. The handle. The warning light above it.
I remember the seal making this soft little click. Ridiculous thing to remember, isn't it?
Not the death. Not the person. The click.
There was shouting. Someone said my name like I had already done something unforgivable.
And then... a pressure drop. Or the memory of one.
Everything after that is gone.
Like somebody cut a page out of my life with a knife.
[HALE RESPONSE PARTIALLY WITHHELD: EMOTIONAL STRESS DETECTED]
Thank you.
Avoiding the obvious horror with paperwork. That's very station of us.
But I need to know. If that file is real, then something is using my signal.
And I don't like sharing. Especially with ghosts.
For the record, I was bad at sharing before the ghosts.
[AUTOPSY FRAGMENT RECOVERED]

events.title: AUTOPSY FRAGMENT: HALE
events.content:
SUBJECT: ETHAN HALE
CAUSE OF DEATH: DECOMPRESSION TRAUMA
LOCATION: DOOR 12 ACCESS CORRIDOR
RECOVERY STATUS: BODY NOT RECOVERED
NOTE: VITALS TERMINATED AT 21:20
ARCHIVE INTEGRITY: 68%
events.caption: Recovered emergency medical fragment.

events.text: Body not recovered.
events.speaker: HALE

events.text: That's somehow worse than recovered.
events.speaker: HALE

events.text: If they found a body, at least the nightmare has a shape.
events.speaker: HALE

events.text: A place. A bag. A report nobody wants to sign.
events.speaker: HALE

events.text: But not recovered means the station either lost me...
events.speaker: HALE

events.text: ...or it found something it refused to call a body.
events.speaker: HALE

events.text: Listen to me. I sound like a man arguing with a filing cabinet.
events.speaker: HALE

events.text: This is exactly why I hated medical logs. They make tragedy sound organized.
events.speaker: HALE

events.text: [VOICE SIGNATURE SCAN ACTIVE]

events.title: VOICE SIGNATURE REPORT
events.content:
ACTIVE SPEAKER: ETHAN HALE
VOICEPRINT MATCH: 84%
SIGNAL DELAY: NONLINEAR
CARRIER TRACE: 7.14 MHz harmonic residue detected
events.caption: Recovered voiceprint comparison from live relay traffic.
events.message: [VOICE SIGNATURE REPORT ARCHIVED TO DATA BANK]

events.text: Eighty-four percent.
events.speaker: HALE

events.text: That is just high enough to ruin my day and low enough to avoid answering anything.
events.speaker: HALE

events.text: Nonlinear delay. 7.14 residue. Wonderful. Even my voice is doing suspicious things now.
events.speaker: HALE

events.text: [TIMELINE CONFLICT DETECTED]

events.title: TIMELINE CONFLICT
events.content:
21:14 — Door 12 opened.
21:17 — Security override accepted.
21:20 — Ethan Hale declared deceased.

Conflict: Active communication signature matches Ethan Hale after recorded time of death.
Secondary conflict: Communication signature contains 7.14 MHz harmonic residue.
events.caption: Automated timeline conflict report.

events.text: There it is.
events.speaker: HALE

events.text: A neat little contradiction with timestamps and labels.
events.speaker: HALE

events.text: The system says I died at 21:20. The relay says I kept talking after.
events.speaker: HALE

events.text: And somewhere in between those two statements, I am supposed to exist.
events.speaker: HALE

events.text: [CORRELATION REQUIRED]
[CORRELATION FAILED]
No. Those records don't line up that way.
events.speaker: HALE

events.text: Try again, but don't force the evidence to comfort us. It will not be kind enough for that.
events.speaker: HALE

events.text: [ARCHIVE REVIEW AVAILABLE]
[CORRELATION CONFIRMED]
So that's the shape of it.
events.speaker: HALE

events.text: A dead engineer with no body. A living signal with my voice. A channel that keeps leaving fingerprints.
events.speaker: HALE

events.text: I would like to formally resign from being a mystery.
events.speaker: HALE

events.text: I can't explain it because I don't know what I am explaining.
And every time you say my name like an accusation, it gets harder to think.
But I am trying. I need you to understand that. I am not dodging this because it hurts.
I am dodging it because every answer seems to bite back.
[COOPERATION INSTABILITY DETECTED]

events.speaker: UNKNOWN
events.text: HE OPENED IT FIRST

events.speaker: UNKNOWN
events.text: NOT ALONE
I heard that.
events.speaker: HALE

events.text: I wish I hadn't, but I did.
events.speaker: HALE

events.text: I remember something now.
There was another voice on the emergency channel.
Not the unknown one. Not exactly.
It sounded... close. Like hearing someone through a wall and realizing they know your name.
It kept repeating one number.
Operator... I don't think we're opening a frequency.
events.speaker: HALE

events.text: I think we're about to meet somebody.
events.speaker: HALE

events.text: [CHANNEL 7.14 OPENED WITHOUT STABILIZATION]

events.speaker: UNKNOWN
events.text: TOO SOON
No— I said stabilize it first.
events.speaker: HALE

events.text: The channel is open now. We did not lose anyone. But we lost control of how it opens.
events.speaker: HALE

events.text: There is a difference between unlocking a door and having something pull it from the other side.
events.speaker: HALE

events.text: [FUTURE TRANSMISSIONS MAY BE CONTAMINATED]
[CHANNEL 7.14 MONITORING ACTIVE]

events.title: SIGNAL STABILIZATION
events.subtitle: Monitoring emergency carrier before opening channel

events.text: Good. Slow is good.
events.speaker: HALE

events.text: I never thought I would say that. I have mocked slow systems professionally.
events.speaker: HALE

events.text: But right now slow feels like a handrail.
events.speaker: HALE
UNKNOWN
events.text: 7.14 IS NOT A CHANNEL
It is a survivor.
events.speaker: UNKNOWN

events.text: Hale is silent for a moment.
events.speaker: SYSTEM

events.text: Operator...
events.speaker: HALE

events.text: That wasn't a metaphor.
events.speaker: HALE

events.text: Thank you.
That is probably the least scientific comfort available, and right now I am choosing to treasure it.
I can hear the relay hum. I can feel the deck vibrating under my boots.
I am annoyed by you, which is historically a living-person activity.
So let's keep that theory alive for as long as we can.
That's a dangerous question.
Not because the answer is tragic.
Because the first thing that came to mind was a coffee machine.
Not even good coffee. Station coffee. Industrial regret in a cup.
Every morning the machine made a noise like a small animal giving up on life.
Every morning I said I would fix it properly.
Every morning I hit it with the heel of my hand and called that engineering.
I miss that. Normal broken things.
A pump. A bad hinge. Someone complaining that gravity simulation made their knees feel weird.
Problems that didn't ask whether I was dead.
Keep me talking. Smart.
If I talk, I don't have to picture a corridor with my name on a death report.
You know what I don't miss? Station management.
I spent three months warning them the relay spine was unstable.
Three months.
Do you know what I got?
A meeting.
Never trust anyone who schedules a meeting to discuss a warning alarm.
If an alarm is warning you, it has already completed the meeting.
Three pieces. Keep them separate.
Personnel file says I was declared dead at 21:20.
Autopsy fragment says decompression trauma, but no body recovered.
Timeline conflict says my signal kept talking after my recorded death.
I hate that sentence. I hate that it is useful.
